recipes.title: Moules-frites
recipes.description: Belgian national dish of mussels steamed with white wine, shallots and herbs, served with crisp double-fried fries.
steps: Cut the potatoes into 1 cm thick fries, soak in cold water for 30 minutes, then dry thoroughly.
Heat the oil to 160°C and fry the chips for 6 minutes until pale and tender; drain and rest.
Pick over the mussels, discarding any that are open and do not close when tapped.
Melt the butter in a wide pot, sweat the shallots, celery and garlic for 5 minutes without coloring.
Add the white wine, thyme, bay leaf and black pepper and bring to a boil.
Tip in the mussels, cover tightly, and cook over high heat for 4 to 5 minutes, shaking the pot occasionally, until all mussels open.
Heat the frying oil to 190°C and fry the chips a second time until deep golden and crisp; drain and salt heavily.
Stir parsley into the mussels and serve straight from the pot with the fries on the side.

recipes.title: Carbonade Flamande
recipes.description: Flemish beef and beer stew sweetened with brown sugar and finished with a mustard-coated bread crust.
steps: Pat the beef dry, season, and dust with flour. Brown well in oil in batches in a Dutch oven.
Lower the heat, add the butter and onions, and cook gently for 15 minutes until deeply soft.
Stir in the brown sugar and vinegar and cook for 2 minutes.
Return the beef, pour in the beer and stock, and tuck in the thyme and bay leaves.
Spread the bread slices thickly with mustard and float them mustard-side down on top of the stew.
Cover and braise at 160°C for 2 hours, then uncover and cook another 30 minutes until the sauce is rich and the beef is fork-tender.
Stir the dissolved bread into the sauce, season to taste, and serve with frites.

recipes.title: Waterzooi
recipes.description: Ghent-style creamy chicken or fish stew with leeks, potatoes and root vegetables in an egg-yolk-thickened broth.
steps: Melt the butter in a Dutch oven, sweat the onion, leeks, carrots and celery for 10 minutes.
Add the chicken pieces, the thyme and bay leaf, pour in the stock, and simmer covered for 25 minutes.
Add the potatoes and continue simmering for 15 minutes until everything is tender.
Lift out the chicken, pull the meat from the bones, and return it to the pot.
Whisk the egg yolks with the cream and lemon juice in a bowl.
Temper the cream mixture with a ladle of hot broth, then stir into the pot off the heat.
Warm gently without boiling so the broth thickens and turns silky.
Adjust seasoning, sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Stoemp
recipes.description: Brussels-style mash of potatoes blended with seasonal vegetables such as carrot, leek or savoy cabbage.
steps: Boil the potatoes and carrots together in salted water until tender, about 20 minutes; drain.
Meanwhile, fry the bacon lardons until crisp; lift out and reserve, leaving the fat behind.
Soften the chopped onion in the bacon fat for 5 minutes.
Mash the potatoes and carrots with the butter and warm milk until rough and rustic.
Stir in the onion, half the bacon, salt, pepper and nutmeg.
Pile onto plates, top with the remaining bacon and parsley, and serve with sausage or roast meats.

recipes.title: Belgian Waffle
recipes.description: Brussels-style yeasted waffle with a light crisp shell and fluffy interior, dusted with powdered sugar.
steps: Stir the yeast and sugar into the warm milk and let bloom for 10 minutes.
Whisk in the egg yolks, melted butter, vanilla and salt, then the flour to a smooth batter.
Cover and rest in a warm place for 1 hour until bubbly.
Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold into the batter just before cooking.
Heat a Brussels-style waffle iron and brush with butter.
Pour in batter and cook for 4 to 5 minutes until deep golden and crisp on the outside.
Dust with powdered sugar and serve with whipped cream and sliced strawberries.

recipes.title: Speculaas
recipes.description: Belgian crisp spiced biscuit served around Sinterklaas, traditionally pressed in carved wooden moulds.
steps: Combine the flour, sugar, spice mix, baking powder and salt in a bowl.
Rub in the cold butter until the mixture resembles fine crumbs.
Add the milk and bring the dough together by hand, then wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 175°C and line two trays with parchment.
Roll the dough to 5 mm thickness, press into a speculaas mould or cut into shapes, and place on the trays.
Press almonds on top if you like and bake for 12 to 15 minutes until firm and deep golden.
Cool on a rack so the biscuits crisp up fully.

recipes.title: Vol-au-vent
recipes.description: Belgian classic of puff pastry shells filled with a creamy chicken, mushroom and meatball ragout.
steps: Roll the ground veal into small meatballs and poach in the stock for 5 minutes; lift out and reserve.
Poach the chicken cubes in the same stock for 8 minutes until just cooked; lift out and reserve.
Sauté the shallots and mushrooms in 30 g butter until golden.
Make a roux with the remaining butter and flour, cook for 2 minutes, then whisk in the poaching stock to a smooth sauce.
Stir in the cream and reduce to a coating consistency.
Whisk the egg yolk with a splash of cream and stir into the sauce off the heat.
Add the chicken, meatballs, mushrooms, parsley, lemon juice and nutmeg and warm through.
Bake the puff pastry shells at 200°C for 8 minutes until crisp and hot, then fill with the ragout and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Stoofvlees
recipes.description: Flemish beef stew braised slowly in dark beer with onions and a touch of mustard-coated bread, served with frites.
steps: Pat the beef dry, season and dust with flour. Brown in oil in batches in a heavy pot.
Lower the heat, add the butter and onions and cook gently for 20 minutes until very soft and golden.
Stir in the brown sugar and vinegar and cook for 2 minutes.
Return the beef, pour in the beer and stock and tuck in the thyme and bay leaves.
Spread the bread thickly with mustard and lay it mustard-side down on top of the stew.
Cover and braise at 150°C for 2.5 hours until the beef is fork-tender and the sauce dark and glossy.
Stir the bread into the sauce, season to taste, and serve with frites and mayonnaise.

recipes.title: Liège Waffle
recipes.description: Liège-style yeasted waffle dough enriched with chunks of pearl sugar that caramelize to a crackling crust.
steps: Bloom the yeast and sugar in warm milk for 10 minutes until foamy.
Mix the flour and salt in a bowl, then stir in the yeast mixture, eggs, vanilla and honey to a sticky dough.
Knead in the soft butter a little at a time until fully incorporated and elastic.
Cover and let rise in a warm place for 1.5 hours until doubled.
Knock back the dough and gently knead in the pearl sugar; rest for 15 minutes.
Divide into 8 balls and press each into a hot waffle iron set to medium-high.
Cook for 4 to 5 minutes until deep golden with caramelized crackling sugar.
Cool slightly so the crust sets and serve plain or with toppings of your choice.
